summary: Tight ’79 show with stellar China>Rider, Terrapin Station, and high‑energy jams
blurb: The set bursts open with a smoking China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue and rolls through a tight first set. The second set shines on Shakedown Street > Samson & Delilah, Terrapin Station and a blazing Alabama Getaway → The Promised Land encore. Brent’s keyboards drive the groove while Jerry’s lead work stays on fire.
review: December 11, 1979 at Soldiers’ and Sailors’ Memorial Hall captured the Dead in a remarkably focused spell. From the opening “Jack Straw” through an inspired first‑set close with “China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider,” the band built momentum that only intensified after “Althea.” The second set is where the night really shines: a scorching “Shakedown Street > Samson & Delilah” segues into a soaring “Terrapin Station,” followed by a fluid “Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance” and a thunderous drum break that feeds into “Not Fade Away” and “Black Peter.” The encore’s high‑octane “Alabama Getaway → The Promised Land” caps a night of tight interplay, vintage Brent funk, and Jerry’s guitar blazing across the board. While some recordings suffer from mix quirks—most notably buried guitars on a few SBD tapes—the performance itself remains consistently strong, with Phil’s bass “phil‑tastic” foundation, Brent’s keyboard wizardry, and both drummers laying down a propulsive rhythm that keeps the whole show humming.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six out of twelve analyses are clearly positive, praising the China>Rider segue, Terrapin Station, Shakedown Street > Samson jam, and Brent's keyboards. Mixed reviews focus mainly on recording mix issues rather than performance flaws, and only one negative comment notes a lackluster moment. The preponderance of enthusiastic language (“smoking,” “magical,” “on fire”) warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with crisp, tight mix; highlights the full set without dropouts and is praised as the superior Miller source.

key_highlights: Smoking China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue that launches the show.
Terrapin Station performance that lifts the second set into a higher gear.
Shakedown Street > Samson & Delilah jam delivering high‑energy momentum.
Alabama Getaway → The Promised Land encore that ends the night on a blaze.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers super‑stylish slide work, soaring high notes and a ferocious guitar tone that stays on fire throughout the set.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid, “phil‑tastic” bass foundation; dominates low‑end and keeps the band grounded.
band_performance.Brent: Praised as "the man" – vintage funk, crisp keyboard wizardry and standout solos drive the show’s momentum.
band_performance.Mickey: Prolific percussion with loud toms that add extra punch to the jams.
